title: macrolobium
intro: Macrolobium is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family, native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Macrolobium include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and treating various infections, such as malaria and typhoid. Herbal preparations of Macrolobium typically involve making infusions or decoctions from the plant's leaves, roots, or stems, which can be consumed as teas or added to other remedies.
